mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening real estate and construction sectors, which historically leads to broader economic contraction and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the 12-month rolling correlation between HOUST trend and 60-day SPX returns is positive, or if rank IC < 0 over any 3-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above a threshold of 4.0%, it indicates accelerating labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day SPX return after trigger is not negative with 70% confidence, or hit rate < 55%, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous decline in industrial production and rise in unemployment signals a synchronized economic downturn with reduced output and labor market slack, which negatively impacts corporate profits and stock prices over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month negative and UNRATE month-over-month positive
failure_condition: If the combined signal produces a positive mean return or if the proportion of negative returns is below 60% over all triggered instances, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A conjunction of negative quarterly GDP growth and declining housing starts indicates a deepening demand contraction, reinforcing each other's negative signal and leading to sustained equity market weakness over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter negative and HOUST month-over-month negative
failure_condition: If the forward returns in the triggered regime are not statistically significantly negative at the 10% level, or if false positive rate exceeds 40%, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained high or rising level of the CPI erodes consumers' purchasing power and increases input costs for firms, compressing margins and future earnings, which depresses stock prices over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the correlation between CPIAUCSL changes and 20-day SPX returns over rolling 5-year windows is not negative, or if rank IC < 0.02, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in real retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 1% signals abrupt consumer demand weakness, prompting downward earnings revisions and negative stock price reactions within a week.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return after trigger is positive in more than 40% of cases, or if the mean return is not negative, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 2%, any additional rate cuts signal that the central bank sees severe economic weakness, undermining investor confidence and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month, despite lower discount rates.
failure_condition: If in the regime (FEDFUNDS<2%) a further cut leads to positive mean 20-day returns, or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 55%, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of housing starts below 1.4 million units indicate a severe housing downturn that drags down residential investment and related industries, forecasting deep and prolonged equity market losses over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the average 60-day return after trigger is not negative at 90% confidence, or if the signal is false in more than 30% of cases, reject.
